recipeInstructions.text: Preheat oven to 350 degrees
Brown ground beef, drain grease, add water and taco seasoning
In medium-sized bowl mix the enchilada sauce, tomato soup, cream of chicken
Let ground beef simmer until water has absorbed
Add taco meat and cheese to each tortilla
Add rolled tortillas to greased 9x13 pan
Pour sauce mixture over the tortillas and sprinkle with cheese
Bake at 350 degrees for 30 min
Combine all ingredients (except oil) in a medium bowl.
Refrigerate for 10-30 minutes.
Heat oil in skillet over medium heat.
Cook chicken and marinade until liquid evaporates.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Place broccoli on baking sheet and drizzle with olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Roast broccoli for 15 to 20 minutes until charred.
Season chicken with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Coat chicken with flour.
Cook rice according to package directions.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Cook chicken 5-6 minutes per side.
Whisk together jam, minced garlic, soy sauce, and brown sugar.
Serve chicken with roasted broccoli and rice.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
Unroll pie crust and press into a 9-inch pie plate.
Whisk eggs, milk, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Sprinkle ham, cheese, and green onions into pie crust and pour egg mixture over top.
Bake 35-40 minutes until eggs are set. Cool 5-10 minutes before serving.
Mix all ingredients in a glass container.
Cover and refrigerate for 2 hours to 5 days.
Uncover and serve.
Follow instructions on pizza crust package. Top with pizza sauce and shredded cheese before baking.
Mix warm water, sugar, salt, and yeast. Let sit for 5 minutes.
Add olive oil and flour. Knead until smooth.
Let dough rise for 1-2 hours until doubled.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Roll dough to desired thickness.
Place on pizza stone or baking sheet.
Spread pizza sauce and sprinkle cheese on top.
Bake for 15 minutes until crust is golden brown.
Brown hamburger and onion.
Brown pork sausage and combine with hamburger mixture.
Add garlic, oregano, basil, salt, tomatoes, and tomato paste.
Cook lasagna noodles according to package directions.
Mix cottage cheese, mozzarella, parmesan, eggs, and parsley.
Place a little tomato juice in a 9x13-inch pan.
Layer noodles, cheese mixture, and meat sauce twice.
Sprinkle parmesan cheese on top.
Bake at 350°F for 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Cut bagels in half and place on a baking sheet.
Spread pesto sauce on each bagel half.
Top with chicken strips.
Add sundried tomatoes.
Top with mozzarella cheese.
Bake for 10-15 minutes.

name: Scalloped Potatoes + Ham
description: Put a pan underneath in case of bubble over.

recipeInstructions.text: Slice potatoes thinly and cube ham into 1/2-inch pieces.
Grease a 9x13-inch pan and preheat oven to 400°F.
Whisk milk and flour together.
Mix salt, pepper, and onion powder.
Layer one-third of the potatoes, seasonings, ham, and cheese.
Repeat the layer a second time.
Add final layer, pouring milk mixture before topping with cheese.
Bake covered for 30 minutes, then uncovered for 30 minutes.
Cook and dice chicken.
Mix bread crumbs and pecans together; set aside.
Mix chicken, cream cheese, lemon pepper, and mushrooms.
Flatten crescent rolls into rectangles.
Spoon chicken mixture onto each rectangle.
Roll up and dip in butter.
Roll in bread crumb mixture and place on baking sheet.
Bake at 350°F for 20 minutes until golden brown.
Cook bacon until crisp. Drain and set aside.
Prepare broccoli florets.
Combine bacon, broccoli, onion, apple, sunflower seeds, and raisins.
Mix mayonnaise, yogurt, lemon juice, sugar, and pepper to make dressing.
Toss salad with dressing and serve.
Cook fettuccine al dente.
Cook bacon until crisp and drain.
Reserve 1 tbsp bacon fat and add olive oil.
Sauté garlic for 1 minute.
Beat eggs.
Return bacon and pasta to pan and quickly toss with eggs until thickened.
Add Parmesan, salt, and pepper.
Garnish with parsley and serve.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Mix hamburger, pork, oatmeal, onion, milk, seasonings, and eggs.
Form into meatballs and place in baking dish.
Combine ketchup, liquid smoke, garlic salt, brown sugar, and onions. Bring to a boil.
Pour sauce over meatballs.
Bake uncovered for 1 hour.
Separate eggs.
Beat egg whites until stiff.
Beat egg yolks in a bowl.
Mix yolks with buttermilk, baking soda, flour, sugar, salt, vanilla, and butter.
Fold in egg whites.
Let batter rest for 10 minutes.
Cook on hot griddle until golden.

name: Mashed Turnips and Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and diced

recipeInstructions.text: Boil turnips and potatoes until fork tender.
Drain and mash to desired consistency.
Stir in milk, butter, herbs, and garlic powder.
Season with salt and pepper and serve.
Sauté onion and celery in oil until tender.
Add squash, apples, and potato.
Add water and seasonings. Bring to a boil.
Simmer about 12 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Blend until smooth.
Return to pan, add brown sugar, and heat through.
Serve topped with yogurt and pecans.
Cook penne al dente.
Sauté garlic in oil for 2 minutes.
Add tomatoes and cook for 4 minutes.
Drain pasta.
Combine pasta with tomatoes, olives, parsley, and basil.
Top with thyme, feta, and parmesan before serving.

name: Chicken Fajitas
description: Don't be afraid to overcook the veggies. Add seasoning to rice.
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lbs chicken breasts, cut into strips

recipeInstructions.text: Cook chicken with spices in oil until golden and fully cooked.
Remove chicken and stir-fry vegetables until tender-crisp.
Return chicken to skillet.
Add lemon juice, hot sauce, and Worcestershire sauce.
Heat through and serve with tortillas and toppings.

name: Feta Fusilli
description: Add red wine vinegar separately if desired.
recipeIngredient: 3 cups green beans, cut into pieces

recipeInstructions.text: Cook fusilli al dente.
Steam green beans and broccoli until tender-crisp.
Combine pasta, vegetables, and red pepper.
Whisk together olive oil, vinegar, lemon juice, oregano, and parsley.
Toss pasta mixture with dressing.
Add feta cheese and olives. Serve with tomatoes.

name: Chicken Fingers
recipeIngredient: 1 lb chicken breasts, skinned, deboned, sliced into 1-inch strips

recipeInstructions.text: Preheat oven to 450°F.
Prepare three bowls: flour, beaten eggs, and breadcrumb mixture with Parmesan, oregano, paprika, and basil.
Coat chicken strips in flour, egg, then breadcrumb mixture.
Arrange on a lightly greased baking sheet.
Bake for 6 minutes per side until crisp and cooked through.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Combine dry ingredients in a bowl.
Combine wet ingredients in a separate bowl.
Mix wet and dry ingredients together.
Fold in chocolate chips.
Fill muffin tins about 1/4 cup full.
Bake 18-20 minutes.

name: Shepherd's Pie
description: Mix Worcestershire and hot sauce together before adding to the meat.

recipeInstructions.text: Cook potatoes until tender and mash with milk and garlic.
Brown beef with onion and garlic. Season with salt and pepper.
Mix Worcestershire sauce and hot sauce, then stir into meat.
Spread meat mixture in baking dish.
Heat and spread both corn varieties over meat.
Top with mashed potatoes and paprika.
Broil until golden brown.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Spray an 8x8-inch pan and spread fruit filling evenly.
Mix butter and cake mix.
Spread cake mixture over fruit filling.
Bake 45 minutes until golden brown.

name: Pumpkin Roll
recipeIngredient: 1 cup powdered sugar, plus more for dusting

recipeInstructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F and prepare a jelly roll pan.
Whisk together dry ingredients.
Mix eggs, sugar, vanilla, and pumpkin.
Combine wet and dry ingredients.
Spread batter in pan and bake 12-15 minutes.
Roll warm cake in parchment and cool completely.
Beat cream cheese, butter, vanilla, and powdered sugar until smooth.
Unroll cooled cake, spread filling, and reroll.
Wrap and refrigerate at least 1 hour.
Dust with powdered sugar before serving.
Preheat oven to 450°F.
Toss zucchini with oil, salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Arrange on a sheet pan.
Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Bake 5 minutes, then broil 2-3 minutes until golden.
Serve immediately.

name: Meatballs (Oven-Baked)
recipeIngredient: 1 lb ground beef (80% lean or half pork)

recipeInstructions.text: Preheat oven to 400°F and prepare a baking sheet.
Mix beef, egg, breadcrumbs, Parmesan, onion, garlic, salt, pepper, and parsley.
Form into 1½-inch meatballs and arrange on baking sheet.
Bake 17-20 minutes, turning halfway through.
Serve with marinara sauce if desired.

name: Brownies
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup canola oil or olive oil

recipeInstructions.text: Preheat oven to 325°F and prepare an 8x8-inch baking dish.
Combine dry ingredients in one bowl.
Whisk eggs, oil, water, and vanilla in another bowl.
Mix dry ingredients into wet ingredients until just combined.
Spread batter into prepared pan.
Bake 40-48 minutes.
Cool completely before cutting into squares.

name: Blueberry Baked Oatmeal
description: Keeps well in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp melted butter or coconut oil

recipeInstructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F and grease a baking dish.
Toast pecans for 4-5 minutes.
Mix oats, pecans, cinnamon, baking powder, salt, and nutmeg.
Whisk milk, syrup, eggs, butter, and vanilla.
Spread most blueberries in baking dish.
Add oat mixture and pour liquid mixture over top.
Top with remaining blueberries and optional sugar.
Bake 42-45 minutes until set.
Cool slightly before serving.

name: Chocolate Chip Cookies
description: For soft and chewy cookies, remove from oven while centers still appear slightly underbaked.

recipeInstructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F and line baking sheets.
Whisk flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
Cream butter and sugars.
Beat in eggs and vanilla.
Mix in dry ingredients until just combined.
Fold in chocolate chips.
Form dough balls and place on baking sheets.
Bake 8-10 minutes.
Cool on pan 5 minutes before transferring to a rack.

name: Pork Chops, Potatoes, and Broccoli
description: Stagger baking times so potatoes, pork, and broccoli finish cooking together.
recipeIngredient: 1 lb baby gold or red potatoes, halved

recipeInstructions.text: Preheat oven to 425°F and grease a large baking sheet.
Toss potatoes with olive oil, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and Parmesan. Bake 5-10 minutes.
Season pork chops with seasonings and place on pan.
Bake pork and potatoes for 10 minutes.
Toss broccoli with olive oil, salt, and pepper and add to pan.
Bake another 10-15 minutes until pork reaches 145°F.
Rest pork 3-5 minutes before serving.

name: Chicken Chow Mein
description: Add additional stock or water if the sauce becomes too thick.
recipeIngredient: Hot chow mein noodles for serving

recipeInstructions.text: Melt butter and lightly brown onion and mushrooms.
Add chicken, celery, stock, soy sauce, and sugar. Simmer 15 minutes.
Mix cornstarch and cold water until smooth.
Stir slurry into skillet and cook until thickened.
Serve over hot chow mein noodles.
Preheat oven to 450°F and prepare a sheet pan with a rack.
Mix mayonnaise, egg, and seasoning blend.
Coat chicken cutlets in mixture.
Cover chicken with panko breadcrumbs.
Bake 20 minutes.
Top with marinara sauce and mozzarella cheese.
Bake 5-7 more minutes until cheese melts.
Mix corn, milk, eggs, sugar, and cornstarch until smooth.
Pour into a buttered casserole dish.
Dot with butter.
Bake at 325°F for 1 hour until firm.
Prepare rice separately for serving.
Brown ground beef over medium heat.
Drain grease.
Add salsa, enchilada sauce, and water.
Cook until sauce thickens.
Serve over rice.
Place pork roast in a slow cooker.
Whisk remaining ingredients together.
Pour sauce over pork and coat thoroughly.
Cook on LOW for 8-10 hours or HIGH for 4-5 hours.
Shred pork with two forks before serving.
Serve alone or on sandwich rolls.

name: Drunken Noodles
description: Thai chilies can be replaced with chili crisp. Top with peanuts and extra chilies if desired.
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp soy sauce or fish sauce

recipeInstructions.text: Prepare noodles according to package directions.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat.
Cook garlic and chilies briefly.
Add onion and cook 1 minute.
Add chicken and soy sauce and stir-fry until cooked.
Add green onions, noodles, and sauce.
Cook until noodles are coated.
Remove from heat, toss with basil, and serve.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Arrange bread slices on a baking sheet.
Brush with melted butter.
Sprinkle with seasoning.
Bake 8-12 minutes until golden brown.

name: Bran Muffins
description: Unused batter can be refrigerated for up to 3 weeks.

recipeInstructions.text: Combine bran cereal and boiling water.
Stir in buttermilk and cool completely.
Cream sugar and margarine.
Add eggs and mix well.
Combine wet mixtures.
Mix flour and baking soda and add to batter.
Fold in optional mix-ins.
Fill muffin tins and bake at 350°F for about 20 minutes.

name: Blueberry Muffins
description: Store in an airtight container for up to 4 days.

recipeInstructions.text: Preheat oven to 400°F and line a muffin pan.
Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
Whisk eggs, butter, milk, and vanilla.
Combine wet and dry ingredients and fold in blueberries.
Make streusel by combining flour, sugars, cinnamon, salt, and butter until crumbly.
Fill muffin cups and top with streusel.
Bake 18-22 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
Cool before serving.
